content: Just as English language teaching uses explicit reflection vocabulary exercises to help students internalize the habit of reflecting on their own writing, we can design explicit vocabulary-level prompting schemas for LLM self-reflection mechanisms that frame reflection tasks around the consistent linguistic semantics of 'reflect', 'refine', and 're-evaluate' to help LLMs anchor their self-correction processes more reliably. This turns a passive linguistic entry into an active design principle for AI agent reflection.
